108.status: Slowly increasing activities each day and said he feels good, according to manager Kurt Suzuki.
Threw four innings (57 pitches) with four strikeouts in a rehab start with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on June 28 and is back in Anaheim rehabbing.
Progressed to catching off a machine on June 28 and has been swinging and throwing without any issues, but hasn't ran on the field yet
Has been hitting on the field, taking grounders and running with no issues. Started a throwing program on June 24.
Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and continues to throw fastball-only bullpen sessions.
Started his throwing program and has progressed to playing catch with a targeted return date shortly after the All-Star break.

Said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. MRI exam on June 8 revealed a torn ulnar collateral ligament. Underwent successful season-ending Tommy John surgery.
Underwent CT scan on June 10 that revealed the fracture and will undergo surgery that will keep him out roughly four weeks.
Participated in light on-field activity in early June but ultimately decided to undergo right knee surgery. Can't return until July 21 at the earliest.
Sustained injury playing with Double-A Rocket City on May 31. Added to 40-man roster because he had an opt-out in his Minor League contract and placed on IL.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Progressed to live batting practice on June 19. Began rehab assignment with High-A Frederick on June 30, allowing one run on one hit (a homer) and one walk in one inning.
Tossed two scoreless innings vs. Washington on June 27, then went on IL two days later. Underwent MRI on June 30. The results have not yet been disclosed.
Allowed one run in 10 1/3 innings over two rehab starts for Triple-A Norfolk on June 21 and 26. Threw bullpen session at Camden Yards on June 29. Could start July 1 finale vs. White Sox.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continued to discuss treatment options. Seeking a second opinion, as of June 29.
Received facet injection to lower back on June 12. Then underwent minor procedure to have bone spur removed shortly after. Set to be re-evaluated within couple weeks, as of June 27.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Optimistic he'll be ready for start of Spring Training 2027. Nearing a running progression as of June 23.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3. Progressing to his first bullpen session on June 23.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Running in Sarasota as of June 13.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.

111.status: Early departed his start against the Nationals on June 30 after four innings with left elbow discomfort. (Last update: June 30)

Sandoval's next rehab outing will be on July 4, likely at Double-A Portland, when he is expected to throw roughly 70 pitches. No decision has been made on if that will be the end of his assignment. (Last update: June 30)
Sharp in second rehab appearance for Triple-A Worcester on June 28, throwing two innings on just 14 pitches. Will make an outing for Double-A Portland on July 1, then join Red Sox on next road trip.
Expectation is he will start a Minor League rehab assignment on July 2, likely at Double-A Portland.
Throwing plyo balls and working out at team's Spring Training facility in Fort Myers, Fla.

111.expectedReturn: Some time after the All-Star break
111.status: Participating in all baseball activities, though not quite at full speed. General timetable for recovery is eight to 12 weeks, and Story thinks he is closer to eight.
After grinding through injury for two months, was shut down by team's medical staff for a time period TBD.
CT scan showed a stress reaction in left forearm.
Making gradual progress in hitting program, swinging off a tee and against a machine.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain

111.status: Was re-evaluated after some light throwing, and manager Chad Tracy cited improving numbers, saying he is “improving but not quite ready to throw yet.”
Started a throwing program in mid-June, but isn't close to a return.

112.status: Threw live BP (two innings, around 40 pitches) on June 30. Next step would be a Minor League rehab outing (likely July 5). Returning before the All-Star break hasn't been ruled out.
Was shut down during recent road trip (June 22-28), but resumed a throwing program (plyo ball work) on June 29 in Chicago, per manager Craig Counsell.

After appearing in three games in a four-day span (June 25-28), Roberts dealt with inflammation. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said the issue is not believed to be serious, but the team felt giving the righty a break was smart.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 4 prospect, No. 95 overall)
112.status: Logged 3 2/3 innings (57 pitches) for High-A South Bend on June 27. Struck out four, walked none and allowed no runs in his third outing since returning to game action.

Diagnosed with stress reaction in bone within neck. Same area and issue as 2024, which was misdiagnosed as an osteoma at the time. Will need to let bone heal before being reassessed and mapping out a throwing program.
Appendix flared up overnight and Milner required an appendectomy on June 27, per manager Craig Counsell. Estimated timetable is 4-6 weeks.
Pitched on June 25 in New York. Being given rest period to help manage recurrence of knee issue that also kept him on IL from April 8-26.
Resumed throwing on June 22. Will continue to be monitored as he builds up, but president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer said Steele is unlikely to return as a starter this season.
Cabrera was injured June 23 stretching to receive a throw at first base. Timeline for his return TBD.
Has resumed playing catch after feeling some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

114.status: Underwent surgery on June 16. Manager Stephen Vogt said the expectation is roughly a six-week recovery. Had stitches removed June 30.
Playing catch and hitting off of a tee. MRI and CT imaging revealed non-displaced fracture after he exited June 13 game against Detroit. Expected to miss 4-6 weeks.
Needs right elbow surgery. Set to meet with Dr. Keith Meister on July 2 to determine full scope of procedure and the next steps.

115.status: Was scheduled to play a full game in center field for Triple-A Albuquerque on June 30, but was scratched before first pitch because of groin tightness.
His rehab progressed to the point that he began throwing a weighted ball on June 30 and he hopes to play catch soon.
Continuing rehab exercises but has not begun throwing.
Will undergo season-ending Tommy John surgery, possibly as early as July 1.
Had surgery June 22 to have an internal brace put in his elbow. Expected recovery is one year.More >
Will receive a follow-up MRI at the end of June, with hopes of being cleared for increased activity.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Underwent an MRI June 16 as doctors try to get a handle on what has stopped his rehab from advancing.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 15 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw 100 mph in his session against hitters in Lakeland, Fla., on June 27. Scheduled to throw next session on July 2.
Completing a running progression along with rehab exercises. Not ready to resume hitting.
Playing catch and completing rehab. Scratched from scheduled return June 21 after suffering a left hamstring strain during June 17 side session. Playing catch and completing rehab daily.

Struck by therapy band below left eye during postgame arm-care routine June 16. Returned to Detroit and is expected to undergo further evaluation next week. Not expected to suffer long-term damage to eyesight. Moved to 60-day IL on June 26.
Began a throwing progression on June 15.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9. Fracture is healing slowly, according to A.J. Hinch.
Completing a return-to-play throwing progression. Transferred to 60-day injured list on June 23.
Started a throwing progression on June 22.
Underwent a hydrodissection procedure on June 10 to address lingering soreness in elbow.

117.status: The consensus is Peña should be able to return around the end of the 10-day IL window.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment June 30 at Triple-A Sugar Land and played first base.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Threw 25 pitches in three innings for the FCL Astros on June 30 in his first Minor League rehab outing since undergoing Tommy John surgery on May 23, 2025.
Will make another Minor League rehab start July 1 for Triple-A Sugar Land at Oklahoma City.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Will make third Minor League rehab start July 1 for Double-A Corpus Christi.
Threw 85 pitches over six shutout innings on June 27.
Started at third base and went 2-for-3 with a double and a walk on June 29 in his second rehab assignment with the Florida Complex League Astros.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Exited fifth inning of June 30 game after experiencing discomfort in right shoulder/lat area. Had been feeling it throughout inning, but alerted staff after pickoff throw to first base. Will be evaluated further July 1.

Experienced setback June 16 that led to injection in his shoulder. Began throwing progression June 22 and is in Arizona continuing that> Hope is that he will go on rehab assignment before the All-Star break.
Suffered shoulder injury while rehabbing foot injury May 6. Threw a bullpen June 27 but suffered a setback and will be reevaluated June 30.
After shoulder setback, resumed rehab assignment on June 24. Threw two scoreless innings at Triple-A on June 27. Scheduled for three innings on July 1.
Scheduled for elbow surgery July 1 with Dr. Neal ElAttrache, but full extent of surgery won't be known until Ragans undergoes the procedure. Likely out for the rest of the season, though.

Diagnosed with Grade 3 (severe) tear of a portion of left plantar fasciitis. Began swinging June 17 and will continue rehab in Arizona at the start of July, with hope for rehab assignment before All-Star break.

118.injury: Fractured hamate bone in right hand
118.status: Sustained injury during at-bat June 13 and had surgery to remove broken hamate bone June 14. Will continue rehab in Arizona at start of July, with the hope for rehab assignment before All-Star break.

Has been battling the left hand injury for over a month and needs extended rest to let it heal. Rehabbing in Kansas City but not swinging yet. Scheduled for further evaluation June 30 to determine a better timeline for return.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Not doing any baseball activities and appears unlikely to return before the end of the first half.
Could face hitters for the first time since going on the IL during the weekend of July 3-5.
Stretched out to 2 2/3 innings on 50 pitches in his second rehab start with Triple-A Oklahoma City on June 30.
Has started playing catch. The Dodgers are hopeful that he will not have another setback.
Nearing the end of his rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City, having pitched a back-to-back and an up-down.
Threw a 15-pitch bullpen session on June 26, his first since having surgery. Expected to need at least four more bullpens before facing hitters.
Imaging revealed no structure damage in his elbow, just inflammation due to wear and tear.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

119.injury: Recovery from right shoulder surgery, lower back surgery
119.status: Underwent a surgical procedure on his back, he shared on social media.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Rehabbing at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.

120.status: Parker most likely will undergo Tommy John surgery. He exited in the bottom of the seventh inning on June 28, after issuing a four-pitch walk in which he appeared in noticeable discomfort.
Experienced left elbow tightness during series in Baltimore, recorded a 10.80 ERA in his last seven outings.
Progressed to playing catch at a distance of 90 feet.
Pitching in rehab assignment with Single-A Fredericksburg.
Continuing throwing progression at Nationals' training complex, playing catch at a distance of 75 feet.
Threw 26 pitches (18 strikes) over two-thirds of an inning in his first rehab start at Single-A Fredericksburg on June 28.
Herz has been shut down after an MRI revealed a left flexor strain following his first rehab start for Single-A Fredericksburg. Herz underwent Tommy John surgery in April 2025.
Threw off the mound for the first time last week, on schedule with throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
On schedule with continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL.

121.status: Began playing light catch in June and is due to throw his first bullpen session late in the week of June 29. Will need something approximating a Spring Training-style buildup.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment with Triple-A Syracuse on June 30, setting him up for a July return. Will need at least a week on assignment, and possibly more.
Restarted a Minor League rehab assignment with Triple-A Syracuse on June 30 and will be evaluated daily over the coming week-plus. Will be exclusively a DH upon his return.
Will reportedly miss 4-6 weeks to the flexor strain, a long-term injury that intensified during a June 24 doubleheader against the Cubs.
Threw 20 pitches and allowed two unearned runs in two-thirds of an inning in his first rehab assignment with Single-A St. Lucie on June 25.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: No baseball activities at this time.
Not hitting at this time due to inflammation in his right hand.
Continuing to rehab and has started gripping activities.
Started a running and swinging progression on June 29.
Started to play catch and a swinging progression during the week of June 22. Will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Began a throwing progression on June 22. A's are hopeful he could return when first eligible on July 3.
MRI on June 1 revealed a strain of the shoulder capsule and subscapularis muscle. Received a PRP injection on June 4 and will be re-evaluated 4-6 weeks from that date.
Began a throwing progression on June 11. No timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Threw a two-up, 30-pitch bullpen on June 23. Threw one scoreless inning on rehab assignment with Double-A Altoona on June 27.

134.injury: Left hand fourth and fifth metacarpal non-displaced fractures
134.status: Cleared to begin gripping activities on June 22. No longer using cast for left hand. Will rehab in Pirate City in Bradenton, Fla., starting June 29.
Removed from game June 24 with hamstring discomfort after running the bases on a double-play ball in the third inning. Diagnosed with strain after ultrasound and MRI.

Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster. Began side progression the first week of June. Expected to throw live batting practice first week of July.

Saw local orthopedics in Indianapolis. Information was sent to Dr. Keith Meiser, who performed his Tommy John surgery in 2022. Ligament is not an issue, per athletic trainer Todd Tomczyk. Will not throw for five to seven days.

135.status: Placed on the IL after pitching through left knee soreness. Manager Craig Stammen said Morgan aggravated the issue when he fell off the mound a couple outings ago and had been unavailable for back-to-back days or multiple innings.
Sidelined after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May, and threw a light bullpen on June 27.
Has made five rehab appearances with a 2.12 ERA for Triple-A El Paso. Is nearing a return.
Experienced elbow soreness after his outing on June 21. Per manager Craig Stammen, "will be down from throwing for a couple days, then get back at it.”

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch in late May and played long toss on June 23.
Began throwing program in late May. Struck out two over three innings in June 19 rehab start with Triple-A El Paso.
Continuing his recovery at the team's complex in Arizona but is not yet scheduled for a rehab assignment.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May, and landed on the IL when it worsened. Team is optimistic his absence won't be lengthy.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Out of lineup for third straight day on June 30 but has pushed to avoid IL and keep playing. Mariners will continue to monitor status. Injured during swing in June 27 game.

Has ramped up running progression and fielding drills in both infield and outfield, though club is expected to wait until end of homestand on July 7 to determine when he'll begin a Minor League rehab assignment.

Slated for followup MRI on July 6, and has moved rehab to club's Spring Training facility in Arizona. Current injury is a more serious strain in a different part of his lat than his injury earlier this season.More »
Slated for followup MRI on July 6. Injury "kind of came out of nowhere" ahead of June 9 game, per GM Justin Hollander. Will undergo another MRI in three to four weeks.
Has begun a throwing program. On a similar timeline for return to Matt Brash and Cooper Criswell.
Pulled off rehab assignment due to pain in his finger. Meeting with doctor, and if injury isn't making progress, he'll need season-ending surgery.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Departed at the end of the seventh inning on June 28. Underwent an MRI exam on June 29 in Arizona, which revealed no structural damage. Out of the starting lineup again on June 30 but available off the bench, likely limited to an emergency situation.
Will continue his running progression this week at the club's complex in Arizona and is set to begin throwing and hitting.
Continuing to rehab with Triple-A Sacramento, set to appear June 30.
Threw a 25-pitch bullpen on June 29 and is set to rehab in Arizona Complex League games later this week.
Departed the Giants' 9-6 loss to the A's on June 25 due to back stiffness. Is rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and could return after minimum 10 days.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: The Cardinals skipped May’s normal turn in the rotation on June 27 as a precautionary measure after he experienced tightness in his back during his last start on June 21. He is expected to avoid the injured list and make his start next time through the rotation.
Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL. He was transferred to the 60-day IL on June 12.

139.status: Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, 2025, with target return around midseason. Faced hitters in live batting practice for the first time on June 30.
Began a rehab assignment with the Rookie-level FCL Rays on June 27, then transferred that assignment to Triple-A Durham on June 30. Started first game in right field, batting second.

Initially sidelined by right shoulder injury, had rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle April 11, then shut down May 12 due to left shoulder inflammation. Started rehab assignment with Triple-A Durham on June 30, playing second base.
Allowed five runs on six hits in two innings on June 23 while dealing with sore ankle. Was placed on IL two days later.

Reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. Should resume throwing before the end of June.

Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation. Threw a 20-pitch bullpen on June 8 but not yet nearing a return to game action.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Advanced from playing catch on flat ground to a mound progression.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he suffered while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 8.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

140.status: Was pulled from lineup before first at-bat on June 30. Club waiting to see how he feels before July 1 finale in Cleveland.
Sustained injury after slamming into wall making a catch on June 28. Held out of lineup on June 30 but is likely to avoid injured list stint.

Left June 29 game with trainer before start of eighth inning. Felt exhaustion after throwing 16 pitches in previous inning, and was pulled after manager Skip Schumaker noticed fatigue. Is not expected to need an IL stint.
Began a rehab assignment with Double-A Frisco on June 28. He’s expected to throw on July 1. Manager Skip Schumaker said he should only need a couple outings before returning.
Was scratched from the starting lineup minutes before first pitch on June 27; landed on IL one day later.
Is expected to be out "a little bit" due to a neck injury that hasn't subsided.
Attempted to pitch through ankle injury before aggravating it when slipping on an on-deck circle making a play. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 23 to remove loose body.
Began rehab assignment at Double-A Frisco on June 21, allowing one hit in one scoreless inning.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but was cleared to begin his progression again in early May. Expected to throw live BP in coming weeks.
Suffered multiple fractures on the pinky finger of his right hand after being hit by a pitch in June 13 game in Boston. Will receive additional X-rays/scans when the team returns home.
Last appeared in a June 9 loss to the Royals, giving up a go-head homer to Jac Caglianone.
Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23. Moved to 60-day IL on May 31.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Late scratch on Tuesday, but Schneider is optimistic Guerrero could return as early as Wednesday's series finale against the Mets.
An MRI revealed a stress reaction (small fracture) in his back, a new injury which will shut him down for a couple of weeks.
Beginning to feel better and has started playing catch again after a recent setback.
Continues to play catch and ramp back up after getting a shot recently to help with recovery.
Sprained ankle after crashing into the wall making a catch against the Rangers on June 26. X-rays revealed no fracture.
Made first rehab appearance on June 16, but was scratched from second planned rehab appearance with a neck issue, per John Schneider.
Began rehab assignment on Monday and went 0-for-3 as DH in the Complex League.
Will begin hitting over the next week or two in hopes he can be a factor in late 2026.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Likely will not play before weekend series in New York at earliest, but club is proceeding day to day with his status.
Will likely make at least one more Minor League rehab start before being activated.
Expected to undergo arthroscopic surgery July 1 or 2. Further course of action to be determined after that.
Removed from game June 28 and placed on the injured list June 29. Will miss “significant” time, per manager Derek Shelton, months rather than weeks.
Threw a bullpen session on June 27 and was encouraged.
Underwent follow-up exam and signs are promising but is still a ways off.
Threw bullpen session June 23 but still faces “a multi-week progression” before facing hitters.
Has thrown four bullpen sessions, could begin facing hitters the week of June 28.
Has begun taking overhand batting practice and caught a bullpen session on June 22. His return date remains uncertain.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.

143.status: He threw live BP at Citizens Bank Park on June 30. Phillies interim manager Don Mattingly said he expects Keller will need a rehab assignment before he rejoins the team.

Reinstated from the restricted list on June 25 and placed on the 60-day IL. Experienced right elbow discomfort while ramping up baseball activities for return from 80-game suspension. Underwent season-ending surgery June 19.
Injured on June 10 after attempting to throw out a runner at the plate. Underwent season-ending surgery on June 24. A free agent at season's end, García is expected to be fully ready for 2027 season.

143.injury: Discogenic pain and facet inflammation in back
143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.

144.status: Allowed one run on three hits over three innings (41 pitches) with four strikeouts in first rehab start with Single-A Augusta on June 30.
Did running drills on June 30. Per manager Walt Weiss, Acuña's return before the All-Star break would be a "long shot."
MRI showed no structural damage. Team is hopeful he can return shortly after All-Star break.
Will likely be sidelined through the end of July, according to manager Walt Weiss, and may not return until August.
Recorded three strikeouts in a scoreless inning while making his second Minor League rehab appearance for Double-A Columbus on June 23. Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Began a rehab assignment June 23 in the catcher position as he attempts to boost his chance of returning to the roster. Has appeared in 19 career games behind the plate, including 15 in 2019.
Evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister on June 15 and prescribed one month of rest. If his MRI is clean when he is re-evaluated in July, will be cleared to begin throwing progressions.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. Was in New York on June 13 for a doctor's visit when Atlanta played the Mets.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.

145.status: Not in lineup June 30 after hit by pitch in eighth inning June 29. “[He’s] a little bit sore today,” manager Will Venable said. “I figure it's a good day to give him a day.”
Progressing but still feeling some concussion symptoms and is slow to ramp up baseball activities, per manager Will Venable.
With team in Baltimore and is likely to start series finale on July 1.
Scheduled to throw two innings July 1 for High-A Winston-Salem.
Scheduled to throw one inning July 1 for High-A Winston-Salem.
On road trip with team and “day to day,” with no timeline set for rehab assignment, per manager Will Venable.
Replaced on the roster by fellow southpaw Chris Murphy.
Pulled from his injury rehab assignment and sent back to Chicago for further evaluation on June 1 after continuing to experience lower-body soreness.
Felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Had been managing the issue, which is similar to the one he had in 2025, all season. Got to the point that he needed to rest it.
X-rays came back negative after taking a comebacker off left wrist to begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Jacksonville on June 28. Will make next scheduled rehab start on July 3.
Injured during warm-up drills on May 31. Timeline for return to play was approximately six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 21.
Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.More >
First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching, just the build-up. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.More >

146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: Manager Aaron Boone said June 30 that the slugger may still be a “couple weeks” away from reimaging on his fractured rib.

Joined the club in Boston to perform baseball activities. Grisham will play in a rehab game July 1 with Double-A Somerset, then could be back with the Yankees on July 3 for the start of a three-game series with the Twins.
McMahon will play in a rehab game July 1 with Double-A Somerset, then could be back with the Yankees on July 3 for the start of a three-game series with the Twins.
Tossed two "innings" of live batting practice June 30, throwing approximately 30 pitches. Will throw another live BP July 5.
Continuing recovery after recent setback. Is moving with a little more intensity and hitting again against velocity, per manager Aaron Boone.
Continuing to log bullpen sessions. Expected to begin facing hitters in July.

147.playerName: George Lombard Jr. (Yankees' No. 1 prospect, MLB's No. 18)
147.status: Left June 16 game for Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre in fifth inning, one inning after having glove knocked off on a play at second. Sustained two sprained fingers on left hand, according to Yankees spokesperson.
Resumed throwing weekend of June 13-14. Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

158.status: On track to rejoin the Brewers before the All-Star break after striking out seven in three solid innings for Triple-A Nashville on June 28 at the start of a rehab assignment. Scheduled to pitch once more for Nashville on July 3 (65-70 pitches). (last updated: June 30)

Departed Milwaukee on June 29 to travel to Nashville, where he is expected to begin a rehab assignment with the Triple-A Sounds soon. Live BP session was scheduled for June 30. (last updated: June 30)
Received anti-inflammatory injection on June 12 in his pectoral minor. Throwing at 60-75 feet as of June 30. (last updated: June 30)
Returned from the IL on May 31 only to feel discomfort during a pregame throwing session and land back on the IL. Was scheduled to throw live BP on June 30. (last updated: June 30)

Recent imaging revealed the development of torn cartilage at his kneecap. Underwent arthroscopic surgery on June 25 that pushed his expected return from late June to late July. (last updated: June 30)
Had been optioned to Triple-A Nashville, but that was reversed when he reported discomfort. Scheduled to pitch two innings on July 3 in the Arizona Complex League. (last updated: June 30)

Exited June 5 appearance warming up for the seventh inning and diagnosed with a partial proximal UCL sprain. Was scheduled for a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister on June 30 to determine whether Tommy John surgery is needed. (last updated: June 30)

Underwent successful but season-ending thoracic outlet decompression surgery on June 22 in Dallas. Based on a typical recovery, Priester should be ready for the start of next season. (last updated: June 23)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 31)
